# Questions in Livestream 203

Live Stream #203: Degenerative Artificial Intelligence

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=693" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:33</a> | How can a late-date Exodus view handle Merneptah's campaign overlapping Joshua's era? | He said the overlap is not fatal because Merneptah targeted places like Gezer while Israel was still concentrated farther inland around Shiloh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=1184" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:44</a> | Were Amorites mentioned in Merneptah's stele? | No. He said the stele mentions groups such as the Hurrians and remnants of Mitanni, but not the Amorites, who were not being treated as Egyptian enemies there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=1285" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:25</a> | Why do you favor the conspiracy-assassination reading of Ay and Horemheb's succession? | He pointed especially to the Dakhamunzu affair as the reason he leans that way. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=1636" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:16</a> | What does it mean to incorporate a Christian worldview into fiction? | He argued for embedding a moral vision in the story itself rather than turning fiction into a sermon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=2004" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">33:24</a> | Are there Egyptian texts that treat Ramesses and Avaris as separate sites, and what would that imply? | Yes. He said they are genuinely separate places, divided by both distance and water, which matters for Exodus-site arguments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=2520" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:00</a> | In Luke 23:43, does "today" mean immediately, soon, or just someday? | He treated the saying as pointing to immediate postmortem fellowship rather than an indefinite someday. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=2709" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:09</a> | Does Exodus 21 support abortion by treating an unborn child as not yet a person? | No. He said the passage concerns an accidental miscarriage case and legal uncertainty, not fetal personhood. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=3059" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">50:59</a> | What is good advice for leading a Bible study? | He recommended reading and understanding the text first, using commentaries, and avoiding a study that becomes shared uninformed impressions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=3369" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:09</a> | What is the strongest argument for the resurrection, and which argument needs the most tweaking? | He said this is outside his specialty, but the early church's resurrection belief from the beginning is probably the strongest line he would point to. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=3593" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">59:53</a> | If ancient Hebrew originally wrote mainly consonants, how do we know how names were pronounced? | He said the issue is not a total absence of vowels but how vocalization is recovered through tradition, comparative evidence, and language history. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=3819" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:39</a> | Why did Philistines attack Israel, and were they trying to conquer, settle, or secure trade routes? | He said the conflict was chiefly over agricultural land in the Shephelah, not trade routes or conquest for its own sake. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=3974" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:06:14</a> | Was Samson burning the fields a war crime because he destroyed wheat and orchards? | He treated it as part of the same agricultural-war context, where destroying crops attacked the resource the conflict was about. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=4119" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:08:39</a> | Was Nimrod a Hebrew name for Gilgamesh, or were they different people? | He said Nimrod is probably a literary composite or amalgamation, not simply Gilgamesh or one identifiable person. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=4287" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:11:27</a> | Why does 1 Kings 16:23 say Omri ruled twelve years if your chronology has six? | He said Omri's first years were contested in civil war; the six-year figure reflects independent or complete rule. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=4432" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:52</a> | What is the major significance of the Dead Sea Scrolls, and are there recommended books? | He pointed to the specialist Discoveries in the Judaean Desert series and suggested Emanuel Tov for text-critical work, while noting it is not a casual topic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=4782" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:19:42</a> | Is the book of Job possibly mytho-history? | Yes. He said Job is poetic and can be read as mytho-history, but that does not mean it did not happen. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=4911" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:51</a> | When Yahweh defeated Egypt in the Exodus, was that a message to other nations and their gods? | He said the question assumes too much; he does not think the plagues were mainly about defeating the Egyptian pantheon, but about a deeper cultural message to Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=5145" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:45</a> | How should faith and hope be distinguished among the divine virtues? | He said faith is trust, belief, and fidelity, while biblical hope is better understood as expectation rather than modern uncertainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=5563" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:43</a> | Is Eve's name related to a second tense form like Yahweh? | He said it is possible and interesting, but more work on Hebrew second-tense possibilities would be needed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=5929" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:38:49</a> | Where are the ethical boundaries for student use of AI, fair use, and cheating? | He compared AI to Wikipedia: it may give a starting point, but using it to do the research or write the paper undermines the assignment and can become cheating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=6372" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:46:12</a> | How should someone help other Christians view topics through the lens of God's holy nature? | He said God's holiness is central, but urged caution: what is revelatory to one person may not be persuasive or wise to press on everyone else. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=6792" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:12</a> | What was the process for Moses to get an audience with Pharaoh? | He said Moses would go through palace handlers and an anteroom, and as an adopted royal family member he had standing to seek an audience. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=6991" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:31</a> | Would Moses still count as royal family if he had left Egypt before his adopted grandfather became Pharaoh? | Yes. He said leaving the country would not annul the adoption into the Ramesside clan, though the status would not be especially high. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=7083" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:03</a> | Does Pharaoh's daughter's adoption of Moses set up his later access to Pharaoh? | Yes. He said Moses had standing with Israel, with the Egyptian court, and with Yahweh, which made his role in the story coherent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=7429" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:49</a> | What are your thoughts on the Gavin versus Dan McClellan dispute over biblical monotheism? | He rejected an early-polytheism reading of the biblical text and distinguished the Old Testament's descriptions of Israelite polytheism from what it prescribes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=7705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:25</a> | If salvation is only through Christ, but those who do not know him can still be saved, what is the point of evangelism? | He said evangelism is not pointless because it calls those who do not know or have rejected Christ into right relationship with God before it is too late. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=7969" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:12:49</a> | Would God offer a second chance to someone who rejected false teachings about Christ? | He said that case is speculative: rejecting only a false Christ without ever receiving the true teaching is a maybe, but embracing a false Christ is different. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=8098" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:58</a> | Is it a problem that Exodus does not mention the pyramids or the Sphinx? | No. He said this is an argument from silence; pyramids were obsolete by the Exodus period and not relevant to the Israelites' location or labor. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=8618" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:38</a> | Who created ideas such as temples, sacred space, and ritual purity? | He said these seem to have been widespread ancient Near Eastern ideas about divinity rather than inventions traceable to one person. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=8744" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:44</a> | Do sacred space and temples still matter since Christians have Jesus and the Holy Spirit? | He said biblical prophecy still anticipates temple and sacred-space language, but repurposed toward praise and worship rather than sin offerings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=9324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:24</a> | Do Jews usually use an early or late date for the Exodus? | He said most Jewish dating is closer to a late date, often around 1312 BC, though some Jewish readers influenced by evangelicalism hold early dates. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=9668" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:08</a> | Did learning Japanese kanji, hiragana, or katakana help with Akkadian cuneiform? | No. He said Japanese writing was much easier and more pictographic, while Akkadian cuneiform lacks those helpful visual clues. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=9904" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:45:04</a> | What would be the scariest thing to find on Mars? | He said life would be scariest because unknown biology could raise major questions about origin, danger, contamination, and epigenetic change. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=10148" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:49:08</a> | Is the Exodus Pharaoh's tomb helpful to apologetics? | No. He said royal tombs present only favorable royal material; sources such as Late Egyptian Miscellanies are more useful for Exodus-related context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=10327" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:07</a> | How can true randomness and God both exist if God knows what will happen? | He said this confuses categories: God's knowledge of an outcome does not negate randomness or determine the event. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=10557" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:55:57</a> | If God knows the outcome of every event, does that determine every event? | No. He said God's omniscience and middle knowledge include actual outcomes and contingencies without necessarily causing each choice. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=10838" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:00:38</a> | If God is outside time, how can he change anything or act? | He said God can intrude into time and history because he is sovereign over time, even though his essence is outside time. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=10928" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:08</a> | What is your interpretation of the binding of Isaac in Genesis 22? | He said the passage is deeply debated; his view is that God required Abraham to offer Isaac but that stopping the sacrifice was always part of the plan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=11705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:05</a> | How certain are we that the Egyptian loan words in Exodus are really Egyptian? | He said confidence is high because the words can be compared across Semitic and Egyptian language histories, with some terms long predating their entry into Hebrew. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=12011" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:11</a> | Are there any more updates on the Thutmose II tomb? | He said no major updates are expected soon because the tomb was heavily damaged by flooding, though the final report may eventually add details. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=12417" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:26:57</a> | Are there restrictions on bringing water bottles to Egyptian sites? | No. He said visitors are expected to bring bottled water, but should keep control of it inside tombs and monuments. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=12588" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:48</a> | Did you see Kip's response about the alleged couplet in Numbers 23:7-10? | No. He said he had not seen it and did not see how the passage would support the claimed documentary-hypothesis point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=12721" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:32:01</a> | Who is more credible, Billy Carson or Ken Hovind? | He said neither is credible and compared the choice to choosing between two poisons. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=12978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:36:18</a> | Did Egypt's sorcerers and magicians have demonic power, or was the serpent sign a magic trick? | He said the Bible does not specify; Egyptians practiced magic, but the method in Exodus could range from tricks to ritual magic or other explanations. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=13197" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:57</a> | Was baby Moses being brought out of the water a hint of Hebrews coming out of water chaos? | He thought that was probably overinterpretation, but accepted it as foreshadowing of the Sea of Reeds rather than a chaos motif. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=13606" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:46</a> | Is there an indicator that Aaron was older or younger than Moses? | Yes. He said Aaron is presented as three years older than Moses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=13655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:47:35</a> | Is it safe to swim with Nile crocodiles? | No. He said it is never safe, though ancient people took risks and more often bathed than swam. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/6DKyKGhC8Q4?t=13791" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:49:51</a> | Why did Ehud turn around at the stone images near Gilgal instead of killing Eglon immediately? | He said Ehud was using the idol setting as a ruse to claim a secret divine message, get Eglon alone, and make escape possible. |
